refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. No multiple obligations are present to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 latency ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency does not compromise remote operation safety.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a real-time data compression and decompression mechanism to reduce transmission time
Establish a prioritized data flow control to ensure critical control commands have minimal delay
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Segment the teleoperation system into modular components with clear interface boundaries to isolate latency sources
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a prioritized data flow control to ensure critical control commands have minimal delay

potential_sources: Interdependence between communication protocol and data compression mechanisms
Tight coupling between control command processing and data flow prioritization
coupling_check.notes: Selecting prioritized data flow control as the DP supports independence by isolating latency-critical command transmission from other data types, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Predictive control algorithms are not selected here, which may be needed if latency targets are not met by communication optimization alone
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including local predictive control as a complementary DP if communication latency alone cannot guarantee safe teleoperation under all conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing network and data flow control within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Data flow for monitoring and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control tasks flow through this interface

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection for data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution manages network and data flow control; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module is broader and includes physical vehicle systems; data flow control is only a part, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control but data flow prioritization is a network-level concern, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, less related to data flow control, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of prioritized data flow control because it directly manages network and data transmission aspects, ensuring critical control commands have minimal delay with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if data flow control logic is partially implemented there.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect containment effectiveness.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment proves weak, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate network-level data flow control from application-level command processing to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of establishing prioritized data flow control logically addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency ensuring safe remote operation. Prioritizing critical control commands to minimize delay directly supports the latency acceptance criterion (p95 ≤ 150 ms).

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if parts of data flow control logic are implemented there, causing diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) with minimal diffusion (2), making it an appropriate boundary for the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, indicating less suitable containment. However, dependency on the Network Link interface and possible partial implementation in Teleoperation pose moderate diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through latency measurements (p95 ≤ 150 ms) is feasible via telemetry data collected through the NGHE Solution interfaces, ensuring traceability from FR to IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which manages network and data flow control, aligning well with the FR's focus on teleoperation latency and safety.

recommended_revisions: If containment proves weak, refine the DP to explicitly separate network-level data flow control from application-level command processing to improve modular clarity and reduce diffusion risk.
Clarify interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to avoid overlapping implementations of data flow control logic.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mapping, feasible verification, and appropriate module containment. Although there are moderate diffusion risks due to potential overlap with the Teleoperation module, the current containment scores and verification feasibility support stopping decomposition at this level. The recommended revisions address interface clarity and modular separation but do not necessitate further sub-FR derivation at this time, especially given the high safety criticality requiring stable, well-verified design boundaries.

revise.why: To explicitly separate network-level data flow control from application-level command processing and clarify interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules, reducing diffusion risk and improving modular clarity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module's high cohesion and containment score (8) sufficiently encapsulate the DP.
Verification via telemetry data is reliable and traceable to the FR.
Potential diffusion risks can be mitigated through interface clarification without further decomposition.
open_risks: Moderate diffusion risk due to partial implementation overlap with Teleoperation module.
Dependency on Network Link interface may introduce integration complexity.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data confirming latency performance (p95 ≤ 150 ms).
Interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Verification reports demonstrating containment and cohesion effectiveness.
